name: Kina Collins
bio: Kina Isis Collins (born March 8, 1991) is a community organizer, activist, and three-time congressional candidate from the Austin neighborhood on Chicago's West Side. She earned a B.A. from Louisiana State University in international political economy and sociology, and attended Carthage College. She has run for IL-07 in 2020 (13.8%), 2022 (45.6%), and 2024 (3rd place behind Danny Davis and Melissa Conyears-Ervin). She declared her 2026 candidacy on October 14, 2025. NOTE: She is NOT an incumbent — the current incumbent (Danny Davis, retiring) held the seat through 2024. Collins has never held elected office. Her FEC ID for 2026 is H6IL07511; she raised $45,770.85 in individual contributions (Oct 2025–Feb 2026), with $2,412.78 cash on hand as of the pre-primary report (Feb 25, 2026). She provided an $8,806 personal loan to the campaign. Her campaign pledges no corporate PAC money.
prior_office: Community organizer; three-time congressional candidate (IL-07 2020, 2022, 2024). No elected office held.

jacket.note: FEC pre-primary report (period: Oct 1 2025 – Feb 25, 2026): $45,770.85 individual contributions; $2,412.78 cash on hand; $8,806.57 candidate loan; zero PAC/party money. No corporate PAC pledge confirmed by FEC data.

red_flags.label: Near-empty cash on hand pre-primary ($2,412)
red_flags.detail: As of Feb 25, 2026 FEC pre-primary report, Collins had only $2,412.78 cash on hand despite raising $45,770. She spent $52,440 with debts of $6,871. This suggests a significant burn rate and thin financial position heading into the March 17 primary.

red_flags.label: Three-time primary loser
red_flags.detail: Collins ran for IL-07 in 2020 (13.8%), 2022 (45.6%), and 2024 (3rd place). She has never won a primary despite three campaigns. The 2024 race she placed third behind Danny Davis and Melissa Conyears-Ervin.

red_flags.label: Resigned from Pritzker campaign over racist recording
red_flags.detail: Collins was a staff member on J.B. Pritzker's 2018 gubernatorial campaign but resigned after recordings of Pritzker making racially insensitive remarks were publicly released.

key_votes.bill: Illinois Council on Women and Girls Act (co-authored 2018)
key_votes.summary: Co-authored state legislation creating the Illinois Council on Women and Girls to advise governor and state lawmakers on issues concerning women and girls. Served as inaugural chairwoman.

endorsements: Center for Popular Democracy (2020 — source: Wikipedia)
Northside Democracy for America (2020 — source: Wikipedia)
Chicago aldermanic progressives: Angela Clay, Leni Manaa-Hoppenworth, Daniel La Spata, Byron Sigcho-Lopez (2024 announcement)

career_history.org: Gun Violence Prevention Education Center / Illinois Council Against Handgun Violence
career_history.highlight: Statewide organization focused on gun violence prevention. Co-founded Chicago Neighborhood Alliance 2017 to end gun violence through civic engagement.

career_history.org: Physicians for a National Health Program (PNHP)
career_history.highlight: Organized for single-payer Medicare for All healthcare policy.
Louisiana-based organizing on gun violence prevention and criminal justice reform. Selected for #Fight4AFuture National Leadership Council (2016-17).
Managed political operations for unsuccessful progressive primary campaign.
2020: 13.8%. 2022: 45.6% (came within 4 points of defeating 30-year incumbent Danny Davis). 2024: 3rd place. 2026: currently running.

career_history.role: Inaugural Chair, Illinois Council on Women and Girls
career_history.highlight: Appointed first chair of council she co-created via legislation.
Early civic service role as CPS student in Austin neighborhood.

policy_platform.position: Supports federal tenant protections, community land trusts, and limits on corporate landlords. Expand Housing Choice Vouchers, National Housing Trust Fund. Affordable housing as a human right.
Supports single-payer healthcare (H.R. 3421). No corporate PAC money pledge. Healthcare as a human right for all, including undocumented residents.
Opposes U.S. arms shipments to Israel. Strong advocate for Gaza ceasefire. End ICE enforcement funding for deportations.
Ban assault weapons, bump stocks, high-capacity magazines. Raise purchase age to 21. Fund CDC to study gun violence as public health crisis.
End cash bail (No Money Bail Act). Legalize/deschedule marijuana. Ban private prisons. Sentencing reform to address racial disparities. Community policing over incarceration.
Supports Ultra-Millionaire Tax (Warren). Raise federal minimum wage to $25/hr. Support PRO Act for workers' collective bargaining rights.
Strongly supports H.R. 40 — Commission to Study and Develop Reparation Proposals for descendants of U.S. chattel slavery.

social_pulse.summary: Strong progressive following built over four campaign cycles. National profile from The Strokes benefit concerts (2022, 2024). Positive sentiment among progressive, Palestinian-American, and gun violence prevention communities. No-PAC money pledge generates grassroots credibility.
